run_summary: Screen 1 flags three Apple WWDC sympathy candidates (HCAT, LRN, VRRM) alongside one open VRNS position. All three new flags meet technical BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, confidence >= 3, unjustified panic, minimal/none threat), but their quality diverges: LRN has the cleanest filings isolation (state funding moat untouched by consumer AI), while HCAT and VRRM carry concurrent company-specific overhangs (restructuring churn, contract renewal risk) that muddy the sympathy-fade read. VRNS holds at day 4 with intact thesis.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
entries.thesis: LRN's revenue is per-pupil state funding flowing through accredited K-12 charter contracts. The legal/regulatory wrapper (state charters, ESSA compliance, accredited transcripts) is the product. Apple shipping a better Siri or Image Playground does not let a family enroll their child or obtain a state-recognized diploma. The modest negative move is a generic edtech reflex, not a filings-supported threat.
entries.what_kills: A state AG or governor citing AI in proposing virtual-school funding cuts; charter non-renewals tied to AI-tutor alternatives — neither in filings or news today.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 4, OVERDONE, minimal threat, unjustified panic. Filings isolation is strongest in the basket: per-pupil state funding + accredited charter contracts are the moat; Siri/Image Playground have zero overlap with K-12 enrollment or diplomas. Five-day move +0.24% (flat) + today -3.28% suggest pure WWDC reflex with no multi-day conviction. However, LRN is part of a 3-flag Apple WWDC basket — basket rule applies, tier to exploratory (6% equity) rather than conviction. LRN's filings evidence (state/ESSA/charter structure) is crisp enough to justify exploratory entry despite the modest 5-day flatness.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis relies on a structural moat (state funding + accreditation) that Apple cannot disrupt, but it does not account for the possibility that state education budgets could shift funding allocation away from virtual schools toward AI-augmented traditional schools if those alternatives become cheaper or more effective.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly identifies that Apple's AI announcements do not directly enable a family to enroll in an accredited charter or obtain a state diploma. However, the critique underestimates a slower, more insidious threat: state legislatures and governors who perceive virtual schools as expensive, isolation-prone, or inferior to AI-augmented in-person alternatives may gradually defund or cap virtual charter expansion. The five-day move of +0.24% (flat) and today's -3.28% drop on modest volume (0.65x) suggests this is pure reflex panic with no evidence of fundamental deterioration in enrollment or funding flows — which supports the bull thesis. The real risk is not WWDC sympathy bleed but political headwinds over years, which are not visible in today's price action and would require specific filings or news evidence to elevate.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
entries.thesis: HCAT sells provider-side data warehousing and analytics that require HIPAA BAAs, EHR integration (Epic/Oracle), and multi-year implementations. Apple's consumer AI announcements have zero overlap with hospital analytics workflows. However, HCAT has real company-specific problems (Project Nexus restructuring, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, repeated workforce reductions) that may be the actual driver of the sharp drop — not Apple. The sympathy-fade thesis is clean from a filings standpoint, but the move's magnitude likely reflects company-specific issues rather than pure WWDC reaction.
entries.what_kills: If the drop is actually about Project Nexus / Ignite migration churn (filings flag both as live issues), the move is justified on company-specific grounds and won't recover. The five-day move being sharply positive but today negative suggests profit-taking on a name with real operational stress, not Apple sympathy.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 3, OVERDONE, minimal threat, unjustified panic on paper — but filings reveal Project Nexus restructuring and DOS-to-Ignite migration churn as live operational stressors. The five-day move (+25.54%) followed by today's -9.59% profit-taking pattern suggests the market may be pricing real company-specific distress, not pure Apple sympathy. Thesis 'what_kills' explicitly flags that the drop may be justified on restructuring grounds, not WWDC. Entry here commits capital to a sympathy-fade read that is weakened by concurrent, disclosed execution risk. WATCH pending clarity on whether Project Nexus churn stabilizes; if filings next quarter show migration progressing cleanly, escalate to WATCH-to-BUY.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
entries.thesis: Verra's product is municipal contracts plus physical cameras with chain-of-custody evidence. Apple's consumer AI does not generate court-admissible violation evidence or hold municipal contracts. The modest drop on the WWDC day is generic 'workflow automation' sympathy with no filings support. Caveat: the five-day move is sharply negative, suggesting the market is already pricing the Commercial Services renewal uncertainty disclosed in the 10-Q — that overhang is real and separate from any AI sympathy.
entries.what_kills: The Commercial Services contract negotiation (a known overhang in the Q) producing materially worse pricing/terms — this would justify the multi-day weakness independent of Apple.
entries.reasoning: Confidence 3, OVERDONE, minimal threat, unjustified panic on Apple overlap — BUT five-day move is -14.52% (not a one-day spike; sustained weakness). Filings disclose Commercial Services contract renewal risk (>10% customer) as a concurrent overhang. The 'what_kills' clause explicitly acknowledges the multi-day weakness may reflect negotiation uncertainty independent of Apple. Sympathy-fade thesis is contaminated by a known renewal uncertainty in the Q. Volume multiple 0.74 is below-average (no institutional capitulation signature). WATCH until Commercial Services resolution clarifies; if the contract renews cleanly, sympathy-fade thesis re-opens.
